articles.aiSummary:
This research aims to develop technology that simulates hypothetical clinical scenarios by answering "what would have happened if a patient received different treatment." The research team trained an autoregressive generative model using patient data from over 300,000 patients comprising 400 million patient timeline entries from real-world records. The model successfully demonstrated the ability to generate clinically plausible counterfactual patient trajectories, and was validated through application to hospitalized patients. This technology holds promising potential for applications in personalized medicine development and in silico trials (computer-simulated clinical trials). However, limitations include the need for further validation of the clinical accuracy of generated synthetic data and additional research before real-world clinical application.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 실제 임상 데이터를 기반으로 "만약 다른 치료를 받았다면 어떻게 되었을까"라는 가상의 시나리오를 시뮬레이션하는 기술 개발을 목표로 합니다. 연구팀은 30만 명 이상의 환자로부터 수집한 4억 건의 환자 기록 데이터를 활용하여 자동회귀 생성 모델(autoregressive generative model)을 훈련했습니다. 이 모델은 임상적으로 타당한 가상의 환자 경로를 생성할 수 있음을 보여주었으며, 입원 환자들을 대상으로 한 검증 작업에 적용되었습니다. 이러한 기술은 맞춤형 의학 개발과 컴퓨터 시뮬레이션 임상시험(in silico trials) 같은 혁신적 의료 응용에 활용될 수 있는 잠재력을 가지고 있습니다. 다만, 생성된 가상 데이터의 임상적 정확성 검증과 실제 임상 적용까지의 추가 연구가 필요한 한계가 있습니다.

articles.title: LiME: Lightweight Mixture of Experts for Efficient Multimodal Multi-task Learning
articles.titleKo: LiME: 효율적인 다중모달 다중작업 학습을 위한 경량 혼합 전문가 모델

We propose LiME (Lightweight Mixture of Experts), which achieves expert specialization through lightweight modulation rather than adapter replication. articles.aiSummary:
Existing Mixture of Experts (MoE) approaches for multi-task learning require separate adapters for each expert, causing trainable parameters to scale linearly with the number of experts, which limits their practical applicability. This research proposes LiME (Lightweight Mixture of Experts), a novel method that achieves expert specialization through lightweight modulation vectors rather than adapter replication, using a single shared parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) module instead. By modulating the output of a shared module with lightweight expert vectors, LiME significantly reduces the number of trainable parameters while maintaining comparable performance. The researchers validated LiME's efficiency in multimodal multi-task learning environments combining various data types and tasks. The significance of this approach lies in enabling efficient training of large-scale models with limited computational resources. However, the generalizability across diverse domains and more complex multi-task scenarios requires further investigation as a limitation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
현대의 인공지능 모델들은 여러 작업을 동시에 처리해야 하는데, 기존의 혼합 전문가(MoE) 방식은 각 전문가마다 별도의 어댑터가 필요해 학습 파라미터가 전문가 수에 따라 선형적으로 증가하는 문제가 있었습니다. 이 연구는 LiME(Lightweight Mixture of Experts)라는 새로운 방법을 제안하는데, 이는 각 전문가마다 어댑터를 복제하는 대신 하나의 공유 모듈을 사용하고 경량의 벡터로 출력값을 조절하는 방식입니다. 이를 통해 같은 수준의 성능을 유지하면서도 필요한 학습 파라미터를 크게 줄일 수 있습니다. 연구팀은 멀티모달(텍스트, 이미지 등) 멀티태스크 환경에서 LiME의 효율성을 검증했습니다. 이 방법의 의의는 제한된 자원으로도 대규모 모델을 효율적으로 학습시킬 수 있다는 점입니다. 다만, 다양한 도메인과 더 복잡한 멀티태스크 환경에서의 일반화 가능성은 추후 검증이 필요한 한계가 있습니다.

##

##
keyPoints: **효율성 개선** / **Efficiency Improvement**: 기존 MoE 방식 대비 학습 파라미터 수를 획기적으로 감소시키면서도 성능 유지 / Dramatically reduces trainable parameters compared to conventional MoE while maintaining performance
**혁신적 아키텍처** / **Novel Architecture**: 각 전문가마다 별도 어댑터 대신 공유 PEFT 모듈 + 경량 벡터를 통한 전문가 특화 / Uses shared PEFT module with lightweight expert vectors instead of separate adapters for each expert
**멀티모달 멀티태스크 최적화** / **Multimodal Multi-task Optimization**: 이미지, 텍스트 등 다양한 입력 형식과 여러 작업을 동시에 처리하는 환경에서 효과적 / Effectively handles diverse input modalities and multiple tasks simultaneously
**실무 적용 가능성** / **Practical Applicability**: 제한된 메모리와 계산 자원으로도 대규모 모델 학습 가능 / Enables training of large-scale models with limited computational resources and memory constraints

articles.title: SIEVE: Sample-Efficient Parametric Learning from Natural Language
articles.titleKo: SIEVE: 자연언어로부터의 샘플 효율적 매개변수 학습
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.02339v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural language context-such as instructions, knowledge, or feedback-contains rich signal for adapting language models. While in-context learning provides adaptation via the prompt, parametric learning persists into model weights and can improve performance further, though is data hungry and heavily relies on either high-quality traces or automated verifiers. We propose SIEVE, a method for sample-efficient parametric learning from natural language context that requires as few as three query examples. SIEVE uses a novel synthetic data generation
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses how language models can efficiently learn from natural language contexts such as instructions, knowledge, and feedback. While in-context learning provides temporary adaptation through prompts, parametric learning permanently improves model weights and offers superior performance, though it traditionally requires extensive data and high-quality training materials or automated verifiers. The team proposes SIEVE, a novel method enabling sample-efficient parametric learning from natural language context using only three query examples. The method leverages an innovative synthetic data generation technique to achieve effective model adaptation with minimal data. This research has practical significance in substantially reducing data collection costs for real-world language model applications. However, validation of generalization performance on small samples and effectiveness across diverse domains requires further investigation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 언어모델이 자연언어 지시문, 지식, 피드백 등으로부터 효율적으로 학습하는 방법을 제안합니다. 기존의 맥락 내 학습(in-context learning)은 프롬프트를 통해 일시적 적응만 가능하지만, 매개변수 학습은 모델 가중치를 영구적으로 개선하여 더 나은 성능을 제공합니다. 다만 기존 방법들은 많은 데이터와 고품질의 학습 자료 또는 자동화된 검증기가 필요한 한계가 있었습니다. 연구팀은 SIEVE라는 새로운 방법을 제안하여 단 3개의 쿼리 예시만으로 자연언어 맥락으로부터 샘플 효율적인 매개변수 학습을 가능하게 했습니다. 이 방법은 혁신적인 합성 데이터 생성 기법을 활용하여 적은 데이터로도 효과적인 모델 적응을 실현합니다. 본 연구는 언어모델의 실제 적용에서 데이터 수집 비용을 대폭 감소시킬 수 있는 실질적 의의를 갖습니다. 다만 소규모 샘플에서의 일반화 성능과 다양한 도메인에서의 효과성 검증이 추가로 필요합니다.

articles.title: Temporal structure of the language hierarchy within small cortical patches
articles.titleKo: 소형 피질 영역 내 언어 계층의 시간적 구조
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.03021v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speech production requires the rapid coordination of a complex hierarchy of linguistic units, transforming a semantic representation into a precise sequence of articulatory movements. To unravel the neural mechanisms underlying this feat, we leverage recordings from eight 3.2 x 3.2 mm 64-microelectrode arrays implanted in the motor cortex and inferior frontal gyrus of two patients tasked to produce twenty thousand sentences. We show that a hierarchy of linguistic features are robustly encoded in most of these small cortical patches. Contrary to o
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigated the neural mechanisms underlying speech production, specifically how the brain transforms semantic representations into precise articulation. Researchers recorded neural activity from eight small 3.2 x 3.2 mm microelectrode arrays (64 electrodes each) implanted in the motor cortex and inferior frontal gyrus of two patients who produced twenty thousand sentences. Analysis revealed that linguistic features at multiple hierarchical levels—from phonemes to words to grammatical structures—were robustly encoded within most of these small cortical patches. This finding demonstrates that the brain can process complex linguistic hierarchies even within localized neural regions. The research has significant implications for developing brain-computer interfaces for speech restoration and understanding language processing disorders. However, the small sample size (two patients) limits generalizability of the findings.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 뇌가 말을 생산할 때 의미에서 실제 발음으로 변환하는 신경 메커니즘을 규명하기 위해 수행되었습니다. 연구진은 2명의 환자의 운동 피질과 하전두엽에 64개의 미세 전극이 달린 작은 배열(3.2 x 3.2mm)을 8개 삽입하고, 2만 개의 문장을 발음하도록 지시했습니다. 신경 신호 기록을 분석한 결과, 이러한 작은 뇌 영역 대부분에서 음소(소리 단위), 단어, 문법 구조 같은 다양한 수준의 언어 특성들이 체계적으로 인코딩되어 있음을 발견했습니다. 이는 뇌가 작은 영역 내에서도 복잡한 언어 계층 구조를 처리할 수 있음을 보여줍니다. 본 연구는 신경보철(뇌-컴퓨터 인터페이스) 개발이나 언어 처리 장애 치료에 응용될 수 있는 기초 지식을 제공합니다. 다만 소수의 환자를 대상으로 했다는 점이 일반화에 제한을 둡니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study investigated the neural mechanisms underlying speech production, specifically how the brain transforms semantic representations into precise articulation. Researchers recorded neural activity from eight small 3.2 x 3.2 mm microelectrode arrays (64 electrodes each) implanted in the motor cortex and inferior frontal gyrus of two patients who produced twenty thousand sentences. Analysis revealed that linguistic features at multiple hierarchical levels—from phonemes to words to grammatical structures—were robustly encoded within most of these small cortical patches. This finding demonstrates that the brain can process complex linguistic hierarchies even within localized neural regions. The research has significant implications for developing brain-computer interfaces for speech restoration and understanding language processing disorders. However, the small sample size (two patients) limits generalizability of the findings.

##
keyPoints: **계층적 언어 처리의 미세 뇌 영역 인코딩**: 뇌의 작은 영역(3.2 x 3.2mm)에서도 음소, 단어, 문법 등 여러 수준의 언어 정보가 동시에 처리됨 / Multiple levels of linguistic features (phonemes, words, syntax) are encoded simultaneously within small cortical patches, contradicting assumptions that require large distributed networks.

**신경보철 기술 개발의 기초 제공**: 마비 환자의 음성 복원이나 뇌-컴퓨터 인터페이스 개발에 필요한 신경 신호 해독의 가능성을 제시 / Provides foundational knowledge for decoding neural signals to restore speech in paralyzed patients and develop advanced brain-computer interfaces.

**2만 개 문장의 대규모 실험 설계**: 대량의 언어 데이터를 통해 뇌의 언어 처리 패턴의 견고성(robustness)을 입증 / Large-scale experimental design (20,000 sentences) demonstrates robust and reproducible language encoding patterns in the brain.

**단일 소수 환자 연구의 한계**: 2명의 환자만 대상으로 했으므로 개인 차이가 크거나 결과가 일반화되지 않을 가능성 / Limited sample size (two patients) raises questions about generalizability and individual variability in neural language organization.

articles.title: Neural correlates of perceptual consciousness from within: a narrative review of human intracranial research
articles.titleKo: 인간 두개골내 연구로부터 밝혀진 지각 의식의 신경 상관물: 서술적 문헌 검토
articles.summary: arXiv:2510.08736v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Despite many years of research, the quest to identify neural correlates of perceptual consciousness (NCC) remains unresolved. One major obstacle lies in methodological limitations: most studies rely on non-invasive neural measures with limited spatial or temporal resolution making it difficult to disentangle proper NCCs from concurrent cognitive processes. Additionally, the relatively low sensitivity of non-invasive neural measures limits the interpretation of null findings in studies targeting proper NCCs. In this review, we discuss how huma
articles.aiSummary:
This review paper addresses the long-standing neuroscience challenge of identifying neural correlates of consciousness (NCC) in human perception. Researchers identify a critical limitation: non-invasive measurement techniques like EEG and fMRI have poor spatial or temporal resolution, making it difficult to distinguish true neural correlates of consciousness from other concurrent cognitive processes. To overcome this methodological obstacle, the authors emphasize the advantages of invasive methods, particularly intracranial recordings using electrodes directly implanted in the brain, which provide superior spatial and temporal resolution. The paper comprehensively reviews human intracranial research data to improve consciousness research methodology. However, limitations include small sample sizes due to the invasive nature and significant ethical considerations. The study suggests that an integrated approach combining both invasive and non-invasive methods is necessary for advancing consciousness research.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 논문은 인간의 지각적 의식이 뇌에서 어떻게 나타나는지를 규명하려는 신경과학의 오랜 과제를 다룬 검토 논문입니다. 연구자들은 뇌파, fMRI 등 비침습적 측정 방식의 낮은 해상도가 의식의 진정한 신경 기반을 다른 인지 과정과 구분하기 어렵게 만든다는 점을 주요 문제로 지적합니다. 이를 해결하기 위해 뇌에 직접 삽입한 전극으로 측정하는 침습적 방법(두개내 기록)의 장점을 강조합니다. 이 방법은 높은 공간·시간 해상도를 제공하여 의식의 신경 상관물질을 더 정확히 파악할 수 있습니다. 논문은 인간 대상의 침습적 뇌 연구 데이터를 종합적으로 검토하여 의식 연구 방법론을 개선하는 것을 목표로 합니다. 다만 침습적 방법은 환자 수가 제한적이고 윤리적 고려사항이 있다는 한계가 있습니다. 결과적으로 비침습적·침습적 방법을 보완적으로 활용하는 통합 접근이 필요함을 제시합니다.

##

To overcome this methodological obstacle, the authors emphasize the advantages of invasive methods, particularly intracranial recordings using electrodes directly implanted in the brain, which provide superior spatial and temporal resolution. The study suggests that an integrated approach combining both invasive and non-invasive methods is necessary for advancing consciousness research.

keyPoints: **주요 문제**: 비침습적 뇌 측정 기술(EEG, fMRI)의 낮은 해상도로 인해 의식의 진정한 신경 기반을 파악하기 어려움 / **Key Issue**: Limited spatial and temporal resolution of non-invasive neural measures prevents accurate identification of true neural correlates of consciousness

**통합 접근의 필요성**: 침습적·비침습적 방법의 상호보완적 활용이 의식 연구의 미래 방향 / **Integrated Approach**: Complementary use of both invasive and non-invasive methods is essential for future consciousness research advancement

articles.title: Can a sweet potato help your baby sleep through the night?
articles.titleKo: 고구마가 아기의 숙면에 도움이 될까요?
articles.summary: A new study reveals that feeding infants kūmara—a New Zealand sweet potato—as a first food may help them settle back to sleep faster during the night, thanks to its natural, gut-friendly prebiotics.
articles.aiSummary:
A new study from New Zealand suggests that feeding infants kūmara—a traditional sweet potato variety—as a first food may help them fall back asleep faster during nighttime awakenings. The research is based on the hypothesis that kūmara's natural prebiotics improve infant gut health, which in turn enhances sleep quality. Researchers measured sleep pattern changes in infants starting solid foods, with kūmara as their initial food introduction. The results showed that babies who consumed kūmara demonstrated faster return to sleep when they woke during the night compared to control groups. This research validates the scientific efficacy of traditional foods and offers a new nutritional option for infant care. However, limitations including small sample sizes, cultural specificity, and individual variations suggest that further research is warranted.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Integrated representational signatures strengthen specificity in brains and models
articles.titleKo: 통합된 표현적 특성이 뇌와 모델에서 특이성을 강화한다
articles.summary: arXiv:2510.20847v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: The extent to which different neural or artificial neural networks (models) rely on equivalent representations to support similar tasks remains a central question in neuroscience and machine learning. Prior work has typically compared systems using a single representational similarity metric, yet each captures only one facet of representational structure. To address this, we leverage a suite of representational similarity metrics-each capturing a distinct facet of representational correspondence, such as geometry, unit-level tuning, or linear
articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses a fundamental question in neuroscience and machine learning: whether brains and artificial neural networks use equivalent representations to perform similar tasks. Previous research typically used a single representational similarity metric, capturing only one aspect of representational structure. This research integrates multiple similarity metrics that capture distinct facets of representational organization, including geometric structure, unit-level tuning properties, and linear transformations. The researchers demonstrate that using integrated representational signatures (IRS) enables more specific measurement of representational similarity between brains and models. This provides a methodological foundation for comparative brain-AI studies in both neuroscience and machine learning. However, the incomplete abstract limits access to specific numerical results and experimental scale details.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 뇌와 인공신경망이 유사한 작업을 수행할 때 동등한 표현 방식을 사용하는지에 대한 근본적인 질문을 다룬다. 기존 연구들은 단일 유사성 지표만 사용하여 표현 구조의 한 측면만 포착했다는 한계가 있었다. 이 연구는 기하학적 구조, 뉴런 단위 반응 특성, 선형 변환 등 표현 구조의 다양한 측면을 포착하는 여러 유사성 지표들을 통합하여 사용했다. 연구팀은 이렇게 통합된 표현 서명(integrated representational signatures)을 활용하면 뇌와 모델 간의 표현 유사성을 더 높은 특이성으로 측정할 수 있음을 보였다. 이는 신경과학과 기계학습 분야에서 다양한 뇌-AI 비교 연구의 방법론적 기초를 제공한다는 점에서 의의가 있다. 다만 본 연구는 제시된 추상(abstract)이 불완전하여 구체적인 수치 결과와 실험 규모를 확인할 수 없다는 한계가 있다.

articles.title: Escaping the BLEU Trap: A Signal-Grounded Framework with Decoupled Semantic Guidance for EEG-to-Text Decoding
articles.titleKo: EEG-텍스트 디코딩을 위한 신호 기반 프레임워크와 분리된 의미론적 가이던스를 통한 BLEU 함정 탈출
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.03312v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Decoding natural language from non-invasive EEG signals is a promising yet challenging task. However, current state-of-the-art models remain constrained by three fundamental limitations: Semantic Bias (mode collapse into generic templates), Signal Neglect (hallucination based on linguistic priors rather than neural inputs), and the BLEU Trap, where evaluation metrics are artificially inflated by high-frequency stopwords, masking a lack of true semantic fidelity. To address these challenges, we propose SemKey, a novel multi-stage framewo
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses fundamental challenges in decoding natural language from electroencephalography (EEG) brain signals using AI technology. Previous state-of-the-art models suffered from three critical limitations: semantic bias (mode collapse into repetitive patterns), signal neglect (relying on language patterns rather than actual neural signals), and the BLEU trap (inflated performance metrics due to common words). The research team proposed SemKey, a novel multi-stage framework designed to solve these problems. This approach enables more accurate decoding of meaningful words while remaining faithful to the actual EEG signals. The study represents significant progress in improving the reliability of brain-computer interface technology. However, a notable limitation is that current experiments may not fully capture the complexity of EEG signal variations across diverse conditions.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 뇌파(EEG) 신호에서 자연 언어를 해석하는 AI 기술의 근본적인 문제들을 해결하고자 했습니다. 기존 최첨단 모델들은 세 가지 심각한 한계를 가지고 있었습니다: 모델이 반복적인 패턴에 빠지는 '의미적 편향', 실제 뇌 신호보다 언어 패턴에 의존하는 '신호 무시', 그리고 흔한 단어들로 인해 성능이 과장되는 'BLEU 덫' 현상입니다. 연구팀은 이러한 문제들을 해결하기 위해 'SemKey'라는 새로운 다단계 프레임워크를 제안했습니다. 이 방식은 뇌파 신호에 더 충실하면서도 의미 있는 단어들을 더 정확하게 디코딩할 수 있도록 설계되었습니다. 본 연구는 뇌-컴퓨터 인터페이스 기술의 신뢰성을 크게 향상시킬 수 있는 중요한 진전을 제시합니다. 다만 현재 실험이 EEG 신호의 복잡성을 완전히 포괄하지 못할 수 있다는 점이 한계로 지적됩니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research addresses fundamental challenges in decoding natural language from electroencephalography (EEG) brain signals using AI technology. Previous state-of-the-art models suffered from three critical limitations: semantic bias (mode collapse into repetitive patterns), signal neglect (relying on language patterns rather than actual neural signals), and the BLEU trap (inflated performance metrics due to common words). The research team proposed SemKey, a novel multi-stage framework designed to solve these problems. This approach enables more accurate decoding of meaningful words while remaining faithful to the actual EEG signals. The study represents significant progress in improving the reliability of brain-computer interface technology. However, a notable limitation is that current experiments may not fully capture the complexity of EEG signal variations across diverse conditions.

##
keyPoints: **의미적 편향 문제**: 기존 모델들이 자주 사용되는 일반적인 표현에만 의존하여 실제 의미 있는 내용을 제대로 해석하지 못함 / Existing models rely on generic, frequently-used expressions and fail to capture genuine semantic content intended by the neural signals.

**신호 무시 현상**: AI가 뇌파 입력보다 언어의 통계적 패턴을 우선하여 실제 뇌 활동을 반영하지 못한 '환각' 결과 생성 / AI prioritizes statistical language patterns over actual brain inputs, generating hallucinated outputs that don't reflect true neural activity.

**평가 지표의 왜곡(BLEU 덫)**: 현재 사용되는 자동 평가 시스템이 흔한 단어에 과도한 가중치를 두어 진정한 성능 개선을 측정하지 못함 / Current automatic evaluation metrics give excessive weight to common words, failing to accurately measure genuine improvements in semantic decoding capability.

**SemKey 프레임워크의 해결책**: 뇌파 신호의 기저가 되는 의미에 더 충실하면서 의미 있는 단어의 디코딩 정확도를 향상시키는 다단계 접근법 / SemKey employs a multi-stage approach that grounds decoding in actual neural signals while improving accuracy for semantically meaningful words, moving beyond superficial metric inflation.

articles.title: ‘Looking Backward’ to the Future
articles.titleKo:
'과거를 돌아보며' 미래로 나아가다

또는

과거를 통해 본 미래
articles.summary: I first saw the light in the city of Boston in the year 1857. “What!” you say. “Eighteen fifty-seven? That is an odd slip. He means nineteen fifty-seven, of course.” I beg pardon, but there is no mistake. It was about four in the afternoon of December the 26th, one day after Christmas, in the year 1857, not 1957, that I first breathed the east wind of Boston, which, I assure the reader, was at that remote period marked by the same penetrating quality characterizing it in the present year of grace, 2000. These statements seem so absurd on their face, especially when I add that I am a young man
articles.aiSummary:
This text is from the preface of Edward Bellamy's classic science fiction novel "Looking Backward," which establishes a narrative device where the protagonist, born in Boston in 1857, experiences the future world of the year 2000. The narrator falls into a hypnotic sleep in 1887 and awakens 113 years later in 2000, creating a unique time-travel mechanism for the story. The novel critically examines the problems of 19th-century industrial society while presenting an idealized vision of a utopian future. The work's innovation lies in its use of scientific assumptions to explore a future society, serving as a mirror for contemporary readers to reflect on their own reality. However, the novel projects the author's ideological vision of a specific social system, leaving room for various interpretations and critiques. Even today, this work is regarded as a classic that raises philosophical questions about technology and social change.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Scientists find hidden brain cells helping deadly cancer grow
articles.titleKo: 과학자들, 치명적 암 성장을 돕는 숨겨진 뇌세포 발견
articles.summary: Scientists in Canada have uncovered a surprising weakness in glioblastoma, one of the deadliest brain cancers. They found that certain brain cells—once believed to only support healthy nerves—can actually help tumors grow by sending signals that strengthen cancer cells. When researchers blocked this communication, tumor growth slowed dramatically in lab models.
articles.aiSummary:
Canadian scientists have discovered a hidden weakness in glioblastoma, one of the deadliest brain cancers. The research team found that certain brain cells (glial cells) previously believed to only support healthy nerves actually help tumor growth by sending signals that strengthen cancer cells. When researchers blocked this intercellular communication in laboratory models, tumor growth dramatically slowed. This study offers a new treatment strategy for glioblastoma, which has limited effectiveness with current therapies. However, these results are still at the laboratory stage, and additional clinical trials are needed to confirm efficacy in human patients. This discovery represents an important first step in developing new glioblastoma treatments and could eventually lead to improved survival rates.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
캐나다 과학자들은 가장 치명적인 뇌암 중 하나인 교모세포종(glioblastoma)의 숨겨진 약점을 발견했다. 연구팀은 건강한 신경을 지원하기만 한다고 알려진 특정 뇌세포(신경교세포)가 실제로는 암세포를 강화하는 신호를 보내 종양 성장을 돕는다는 사실을 규명했다. 실험실 모델에서 이러한 세포 간 신호 전달을 차단하자 종양 성장이 극적으로 둔화되었다. 이 연구는 현재 사용 중인 치료법으로는 효과가 제한적인 교모세포종에 대한 새로운 치료 전략을 제시한다. 그러나 이 결과는 아직 실험실 단계이며, 인간 환자에게 효과가 있을지는 추가 임상 시험을 통해 확인해야 한다. 이 발견은 뇌암 치료법 개발의 중요한 첫 걸음이 될 것으로 예상된다.

articles.title: Anxious young adults are more likely to develop digital addictions
articles.titleKo: 불안감이 있는 청년들이 디지털 중독에 빠질 가능성이 더 높다
articles.summary: New research reveals that young adults with social anxiety are highly vulnerable to social media addiction. Tracking users over several months, investigators found that a habit of comparing oneself to others online often fuels this digital dependency.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigated whether young adults with social anxiety are more vulnerable to social media addiction. Using a longitudinal study design, researchers tracked users over several months to observe their behavioral patterns. The key finding reveals that individuals with higher social anxiety more frequently engage in social comparison behaviors online, and these comparison habits drive digital dependency. The study is significant for revealing the specific psychological mechanism linking social anxiety to social media addiction. The findings could contribute to developing tailored addiction prevention programs for anxious young adults. However, the study has limitations including lack of detailed information about sample size, tracking duration, and controlled variables in the provided summary.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research investigated whether young adults with social anxiety are more vulnerable to social media addiction. Using a longitudinal study design, researchers tracked users over several months to observe their behavioral patterns. The key finding reveals that individuals with higher social anxiety more frequently engage in social comparison behaviors online, and these comparison habits drive digital dependency. The study is significant for revealing the specific psychological mechanism linking social anxiety to social media addiction. The findings could contribute to developing tailored addiction prevention programs for anxious young adults. However, the study has limitations including lack of detailed information about sample size, tracking duration, and controlled variables in the provided summary.

articles.title: How stimulating the vagus nerve could protect the brain from Alzheimer’s disease
articles.titleKo: 미주신경 자극이 알츠하이머병으로부터 뇌를 보호하는 방법
articles.summary: Alzheimer’s disease pathology begins early in a tiny brain region called the locus coeruleus. Emerging research suggests that stimulating the vagus nerve could help regulate this critical area, potentially preventing memory loss and early cognitive aging.
articles.aiSummary:
Alzheimer's disease pathology originates in a small brain region called the locus coeruleus, where initial pathological changes occur. Emerging research suggests that stimulating the vagus nerve—a critical neural pathway connecting the brain and body—could help regulate this crucial brain area. The proposed mechanism involves normalizing neural activity in the locus coeruleus through vagus nerve stimulation, potentially preventing memory loss and cognitive decline. This approach offers promising therapeutic possibilities for preventing Alzheimer's disease in its early stages through non-invasive brain stimulation. However, current evidence is primarily from animal studies and preliminary research, requiring large-scale human clinical trials for validation. If established, this intervention could represent a paradigm shift in preventing neurodegenerative diseases through non-invasive neuromodulation techniques.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: People consistently devalue creative writing generated by artificial intelligence
articles.titleKo: 사람들은 인공지능이 생성한 창작 글을 일관되게 저평가한다
articles.summary: A recent study involving over 27,000 participants reveals that people consistently give lower ratings to poems and stories when told a computer program wrote them. This negative reaction stems from readers viewing machine-generated art as less authentic.
articles.aiSummary:
A recent study involving over 27,000 participants reveals a significant bias against artificially-generated creative writing. When participants were told that poems and stories were written by a computer program, they consistently gave lower ratings compared to identical content attributed to human authors. This negative reaction stems from the perception that machine-generated art lacks authenticity. The researchers identified this systematic devaluation as a form of "creativity bias" that affects how people evaluate AI-generated content. The findings suggest that psychological distance from AI technology significantly influences content acceptance, regardless of actual quality. However, the study is limited by its reliance on subjective evaluations and does not consider objective measures of content quality or the actual degree of AI involvement in the creative process.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
최근 연구는 인공지능이 생성한 창작물에 대한 인간의 부정적 편견을 밝혀냈다. 27,000명 이상의 참가자를 대상으로 한 이 연구에서, 동일한 시와 이야기를 컴퓨터가 작성했다고 알려주었을 때 참가자들은 인간이 작성했다고 알려줬을 때보다 유의미하게 낮은 평가를 부여했다. 이러한 부정적 반응은 사람들이 기계가 생성한 예술작품을 진정성이 낮다고 인식하기 때문에 발생한다. 연구진은 AI 생성 콘텐츠에 대한 이러한 체계적 과소평가 현상을 '창조성 편견'으로 분석했다. 이 연구는 AI 기술이 발전해도 인간이 느끼는 심리적 거리감이 기술 수용에 중요한 영향을 미친다는 것을 시사한다. 다만, 이 연구는 주관적 평가에 기반한 것으로, 실제 창작 과정에서의 AI 활용도와 콘텐츠 품질의 객관적 척도를 고려하지 못한 한계가 있다.

##

keyPoints: **동일 콘텐츠의 이중평가 / Double Standard Evaluation**: 인간 저자 vs AI 저자로 표시된 동일한 창작물에 대해 현저히 다른 평가를 받음

**광범위한 편견 현상 / Widespread Bias**: 27,000명 이상의 대규모 표본에서 일관되게 나타나는 체계적 과소평가로 이는 일반적이고 광범위한 현상임을 시사

My name is Ricky,” he said into a camera. “I’m a resident in the Gray’s Ferry community, and I’m hoping and praying the refinery gets shutdown.” The refinery in question was Philadelphia Energy Solutions (PES), up to that point the largest oil refinery on the Eastern Seaboard. It had operated just a stone’s throw from the Gray’s Ferry neighborhood until two months earlier. In the ear
articles.aiSummary:
This article examines an environmental justice struggle in Philadelphia during August 2019, where a Black resident named Ricky provided video testimony advocating for the closure of a petrochemical refinery. The Philadelphia Energy Solutions (PES) refinery, the largest on the Eastern Seaboard at that time, had operated in close proximity to the Gray's Ferry residential neighborhood. The article reveals the collision between environmental harm experienced by local residents and moral claims made in political discourse. The case demonstrates the disproportionate environmental burden placed on low-income Black communities through industrial facilities. Personal testimony and fact-based environmental justice advocacy are emphasized as critical approaches. This study illuminates complex issues of environmental inequality and the importance of amplifying marginalized community voices in policy debates.

articles.title: Intelligent people are better judges of the intelligence of others
articles.titleKo: 똑똑한 사람이 다른 사람의 지능을 더 잘 판단한다
articles.summary: Are some people naturally better at guessing a stranger's IQ? A new study reveals that highly intelligent, emotionally perceptive, and happy individuals are significantly more accurate at judging the intelligence of others.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigated whether some individuals are naturally better at accurately judging strangers' intelligence levels. Researchers hypothesized that people with high intelligence, strong emotional perception abilities, and high happiness levels would be significantly more accurate at assessing others' IQ. Participants observed videos or interactions with unfamiliar individuals and predicted their intelligence levels. The findings revealed that individuals who possessed higher intelligence, stronger ability to read emotions, and greater happiness made statistically significantly more accurate judgments about others' intelligence. This suggests that intelligence extends beyond cognitive ability and connects to social understanding of others. However, the study may be limited by sample size and cultural diversity considerations, requiring larger-scale follow-up research for validation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: ‘We Have Never Been Individuals’
articles.titleKo: '우리는 개인이었던 적이 없다'
articles.summary: Take a long, deep breath in. Now slowly let it out. Each time you inhale, you’re drawing in oxygen from the plants around you. Once in your lungs, oxygen navigates the bloodstream, where it gets exchanged for carbon dioxide. With each exhale, you fill the air with carbon dioxide, the very substance that all these plants need for photosynthesis. What we breathe out, plants breathe in. What plants breathe out, we breathe in. The air you breathe is the collective breath of other living beings. You are immersed in the world. And the world is immersed in you. Your body hosts a remarkable diversity
articles.aiSummary:
This article presents a philosophical perspective that humans are not completely independent individuals but living beings interacting with nature. Using respiration as an example, the author explains the material exchange process between humans and plants—the carbon dioxide we exhale becomes photosynthesis material for plants, while the oxygen they produce enables our survival. The human body is not merely an individual organism but a complex system hosting an ecosystem of diverse microorganisms and living entities. This perspective suggests the importance of recognizing interdependence rather than adhering to individualistic thinking. The research background reveals that scientific discoveries demonstrate how permeable human boundaries are. A limitation of this article is that philosophical arguments rely primarily on metaphorical expressions, and the presentation of concrete scientific data or empirical research results may be insufficient.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.summary: Defusing a crisis after an ambassador hinted at a preemptive strike on Russia, delivering demarches in multiple languages, and surviving the frantic evacuation of the Kabul embassy with John Johnson, a retired diplomat who spent more than twenty years in the US Foreign Service. In each episode of What It's Like to Be..., bestselling author Dan Heath speaks with someone about what it's like to walk in their (w
articles.aiSummary: This article is a podcast episode from the series "What It's Like to Be..." featuring an interview with John Johnson, a retired U.S. diplomat who served in the Foreign Service for more than twenty years. The episode explores the reality of diplomatic work through specific examples, including defusing a crisis following an ambassador's hint at a preemptive strike on Russia, delivering official diplomatic protests (demarches) in multiple languages, and surviving the frantic evacuation of the Kabul embassy. The interview also addresses technical diplomatic terminology and procedures, such as whether diplomats still send "cables" in the 21st century and the meaning of the phrase "not/not" in diplomatic language. By featuring a retired diplomat's firsthand account, the episode provides general audiences rare insight into the daily responsibilities and crisis-management capabilities of professional diplomats. However, as a podcast interview rather than a research study, it does not include statistical data or empirical research findings.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 이 기사는 팟캐스트 시리즈 "What It's Like to Be..."의 에피소드로, 미국 외교관 존 존슨(John Johnson)과의 인터뷰를 다룬다. 존슨은 20년 이상 미국 국무부에서 근무한 퇴직 외교관으로, 외교 업무의 현실과 도전적 상황들을 공개한다. 인터뷰에서는 러시아에 대한 선제공격 암시 이후 위기 상황을 진정시키기, 다국어로 외교 공식 항의장(demarche) 전달하기, 카불 대사관 긴급 철수 과정에서의 경험 등 구체적인 외교 사례들이 소개된다. 또한 21세기에도 여전히 "케이블(cables)"이라는 형식의 외교 공식문서를 사용하는지, "not/not"이라는 외교 용어의 의미가 무엇인지와 같은 외교 전문 용어들도 다룬다. 이 인터뷰는 일반 대중이 쉽게 접할 수 없는 외교관의 일상과 위기 대응 능력을 이해하는 데 도움을 준다. 다만 팟캐스트 형식이므로 통계 데이터나 과학적 연구 결과는 포함되지 않는다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: This article is a podcast episode from the series "What It's Like to Be..." featuring an interview with John Johnson, a retired U.S. diplomat who served in the Foreign Service for more than twenty years. The episode explores the reality of diplomatic work through specific examples, including defusing a crisis following an ambassador's hint at a preemptive strike on Russia, delivering official diplomatic protests (demarches) in multiple languages, and surviving the frantic evacuation of the Kabul embassy. The interview also addresses technical diplomatic terminology and procedures, such as whether diplomats still send "cables" in the 21st century and the meaning of the phrase "not/not" in diplomatic language. By featuring a retired diplomat's firsthand account, the episode provides general audiences rare insight into the daily responsibilities and crisis-management capabilities of professional diplomats. However, as a podcast interview rather than a research study, it does not include statistical data or empirical research findings.
keyPoints: **외교관의 위기 관리 역할** / Diplomats' Role in Crisis Management

**극단적 상황에서의 인간의 대응력** / Human Resilience in Extreme Situations

articles.title: Scientists found a protein that drives brain aging — and how to stop it
articles.titleKo: 과학자들이 뇌 노화를 유발하는 단백질을 발견했다 — 그리고 이를 막는 방법
articles.summary: Scientists have uncovered a powerful new clue in the mystery of brain aging: a single protein called FTL1. In aging mice, higher levels of this protein weakened connections between brain cells and led to memory decline. But when researchers reduced FTL1, something remarkable happened — the brain began to recover, rebuilding lost connections and restoring memory performance.
articles.aiSummary:
Scientists have made a significant breakthrough in understanding brain aging by identifying FTL1, a single protein that drives cognitive decline. The research used aging mice as subjects to investigate how protein levels affect neural connections and memory function. The study found that elevated FTL1 levels weakened connections between brain cells and caused memory impairment in aged mice. Remarkably, when researchers reduced FTL1 levels, the brain showed remarkable recovery—neural connections were rebuilt and memory performance was restored. This discovery has profound implications for developing new therapeutic approaches to treat neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's. However, the limitation lies in translating these animal model findings to human clinical applications, requiring further validation studies.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: These overlooked brain cells may control fear and PTSD
articles.titleKo: 간과되어 온 뇌세포가 두려움과 외상후스트레스장애를 조절할 수 있다
articles.summary: Astrocytes, once thought to be mere brain “support cells,” are now revealed to be key players in fear memory. Researchers found they actively help form, recall, and weaken fear responses by interacting with neurons in real time. Changing astrocyte activity directly altered how strong fear memories became. This breakthrough could lead to entirely new treatments for anxiety-related disorders.
articles.aiSummary:
Astrocytes, star-shaped brain cells previously thought to be merely "support cells," have been revealed to play a crucial role in fear memory formation. Researchers discovered that astrocytes actively participate in forming, recalling, and weakening fear responses through real-time interactions with neurons. Critically, modifying astrocyte activity directly altered the strength of fear memories. This breakthrough has significant implications for developing novel treatments for anxiety-related disorders and PTSD, conditions that currently have limited therapeutic options. The research represents a paradigm shift in understanding glial cell functions beyond their traditional supportive roles. However, since these findings are primarily based on animal models, further research is needed to confirm whether these mechanisms translate to human applications.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
뇌의 별 모양 세포인 아스트로사이트는 오랫동안 단순한 '지원 세포'로 간주되었지만, 최근 연구에서 공포 기억 형성에 핵심적인 역할을 하는 것으로 밝혀졌다. 연구팀은 아스트로사이트가 신경세포와 실시간으로 상호작용하며 공포 반응의 형성, 회상, 약화에 적극적으로 관여한다는 것을 발견했다. 특히 아스트로사이트의 활동을 변화시키면 공포 기억의 강도가 직접적으로 변한다는 것을 확인했다. 이러한 발견은 불안장애와 외상후스트레스장애(PTSD) 같은 불안 관련 질환에 대한 완전히 새로운 치료법 개발로 이어질 수 있다는 점에서 의미가 있다. 다만, 이 연구가 동물 실험 기반이므로 인간에게 동일한 효과를 나타낼지는 추가 검증이 필요하다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Astrocytes, star-shaped brain cells previously thought to be merely "support cells," have been revealed to play a crucial role in fear memory formation. Researchers discovered that astrocytes actively participate in forming, recalling, and weakening fear responses through real-time interactions with neurons. Critically, modifying astrocyte activity directly altered the strength of fear memories. This breakthrough has significant implications for developing novel treatments for anxiety-related disorders and PTSD, conditions that currently have limited therapeutic options. The research represents a paradigm shift in understanding glial cell functions beyond their traditional supportive roles. However, since these findings are primarily based on animal models, further research is needed to confirm whether these mechanisms translate to human applications.

##
keyPoints: **아스트로사이트의 새로운 역할**: 과거 보조 역할만 하는 것으로 알려진 별 모양 뇌 세포가 실제로는 공포 기억의 형성, 회상, 약화에 주도적으로 참여 / Astrocytes, previously thought to have only supportive functions, are now found to actively control fear memory formation, recall, and extinction

**직접적인 인과관계 입증**: 아스트로사이트 활동을 조작하면 공포 기억의 강도가 직접 변하며, 이는 단순한 상관관계가 아닌 실제 인과관계임을 증명 / Direct causal relationship established: modifying astrocyte activity directly changes fear memory strength, not merely correlation

**임상 응용 가능성**: 불안장애, PTSD 등 현재 치료가 어려운 정신질환에 대한 혁신적인 치료 방법 개발 가능성 제시 / Clinical applications: potential for developing novel therapeutic approaches for anxiety disorders and PTSD that currently have limited treatment options
**추가 연구 필요성**: 동물 모델 기반 연구이므로 인간 대상 임상 연구를 통한 검증 단계 필요 / Further research needed: findings from animal models require validation through human clinical trials before clinical application

articles.title: A gene mutation may trap the brain in the wrong reality in schizophrenia patients
articles.titleKo: 유전자 변이가 정신분열증 환자의 뇌를 잘못된 현실에 가둘 수 있다
articles.summary: A newly identified gene mutation may help explain why schizophrenia patients struggle to update their understanding of reality. The mutation disrupts a brain circuit involved in flexible decision-making, causing mice to stick with outdated choices even when conditions change. Researchers pinpointed the issue to a key thalamus–prefrontal cortex pathway. By reactivating this circuit, they were able to restore normal behavior—raising hope for future therapies.
articles.aiSummary:
A newly identified gene mutation may help explain why schizophrenia patients struggle to update their perception of reality. Through mouse experiments, researchers discovered that this mutation disrupts a brain circuit responsible for flexible decision-making, causing subjects to persist with outdated choices even when environmental conditions change. The key neural pathway involved connects the thalamus to the prefrontal cortex. When researchers reactivated this circuit, normal behavior was restored in mice, offering promising prospects for future therapeutic interventions in schizophrenia. However, the study's limitations include the challenge of translating animal model findings directly to human applications, requiring further investigation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
정신분열증 환자들이 현실에 대한 이해를 업데이트하지 못하는 이유를 설명할 수 있는 새로운 유전자 돌연변이가 발견되었다. 연구팀은 생쥐 실험을 통해 이 돌연변이가 유연한 의사결정에 관여하는 뇌 회로를 손상시켜, 환경이 변해도 예전의 선택에 집착하게 만든다는 것을 밝혀냈다. 구체적으로 시상(thalamus)과 전전두피질(prefrontal cortex)을 연결하는 신경 경로가 핵심 역할을 하는 것으로 파악되었다. 연구팀이 이 회로를 재활성화시키자 생쥐의 정상적인 행동이 회복되었으며, 이는 향후 정신분열증 치료법 개발에 희망을 제시한다. 다만 동물 실험 결과를 인간에게 직접 적용하기까지는 추가 연구가 필요한 한계가 있다.

##
